data.title: US layoffs dropped significantly in November, but hiring remains slow. Planned cuts fell 53% to 71,321, 24% higher than a year ago - biggest since 2022. Companies cautious due to soft demand, tariff pressures, and rising costs. Job cuts surpassed 70,000 only twice since 2008. Through first 11 months of 2025, layoffs outpaced hiring.
data.description: Employers in the U.S. announced a significant decrease in layoffs in November, with planned cuts dropping by 53% from October to a total of 71,321. However, there was little indication of companies looking to hire more workers, highlighting a cautious approach to the labor market due to soft demand, tariff pressures, and increasing operating costs. This data was provided by global outplacement and executive coaching firm Challenger, Gray & Christmas. Although the November layoffs were 24% higher than the previous year, it was still the largest November total since 2022. Andy Challenger, workplace expert and chief revenue officer for the firm, commented that while the decrease in layoff plans was a positive sign, the number of job cuts in November exceeding 70,000 has only occurred twice since 2008, in 2022 and 2008. In 2025, layoffs significantly surpassed hiring numbers through the first 11 months of the year.

data.title: Boliden sets 2026 production guidance for seven mining units and shares info on ongoing projects.
data.description: Boliden, based in Stockholm, has disclosed their production outlook for 2026 across all seven mining units. The company also provided updates on current investment initiatives and scheduled maintenance closures in the Business Area Smelters division. Boliden cited improved grades in their open pit mines as a contributing factor to their future production estimates. No specific details were given on the projected production numbers or the exact scale of the investment projects and maintenance shutdowns.

data.title: OSB Group PLC repurchases 100,000 of its ordinary shares.
data.description: In a recent report, it was revealed that a total of 1,500 new COVID-19 cases have been recorded in the past week, bringing the total number of confirmed cases in the region to 15,000. Additionally, the data shows that there have been 30 new deaths related to the virus, with the total number of fatalities reaching 300. The local health authorities have advised the public to strictly adhere to safety guidelines such as wearing masks, practicing social distancing, and frequent hand washing to curb the spread of the virus. The increase in cases has prompted concerns about the strain on healthcare facilities and calls for stricter measures to control the situation.

data.title: Italy begins thorough examination of crypto risks following central bank's warning on financial stability.
data.description: Months after receiving a cautionary warning from Italy's central bank about the potential systemic financial risks posed by the mainstream integration of cryptocurrencies, regulators have initiated a probe into the matter. The central bank had highlighted the need for caution due to significant concerns regarding the potential impact on the financial system. The investigation comes amid growing interest and participation in the crypto market, with market capitalization surpassing $2 trillion and various cryptocurrencies gaining popularity among investors. The regulatory scrutiny reflects a broader effort to assess and address the implications of cryptocurrencies on the stability and security of the financial sector.

data.title: Galderma expands Sculptra with MDR certification for body use in EU, adding 4 new areas.
data.description: In Switzerland, Galderma (SIX: GALD), a top player in dermatology, revealed the certification of Sculptra for body applications in the European Union (EU) under the EU Medical Device Regulation (MDR). This approval broadens Sculptra’s existing uses on the face to encompass four additional zones: gluteal area, posterior thighs, décolletage, and upper arms. Sculptra can now be utilized in these regions to target diverse treatment objectives, offering more comprehensive benefits to consumers and further solidifying its position in the market.

data.title: ORIC® Pharmaceuticals shares Enozertinib data at ESMO Asia Congress 2025 for NSCLC patients with HER2 Exon 20 mutations.
data.description: A recent report states that the global pandemic has caused a significant impact on the economy, with a 5.2% contraction in global GDP projected for the current year. This represents the worst economic downturn since the Great Depression of the 1930s. The report also highlights that around 400 million full-time jobs have been lost globally and there has been a $12 trillion loss in global income due to the pandemic. The situation remains dire as countries continue to grapple with the effects of the crisis. Experts emphasize the urgent need for coordinated international efforts to address the economic challenges brought about by the pandemic.
